facebook.content_angle: Flip turn technique — how most competitive swimmers lose 0.5–1 second at every wall and the three-step fix to get that time back
facebook.post_text:
🔥 You're losing half a second at every single wall — and you don't even know it.

If you've ever pushed off the wall and felt momentum just... disappear — that slight lag, that loss of drive — your flip turn is quietly costing you in every race.

Here's what the fastest swimmers do differently at the wall:
👉 They initiate the flip with the chin — not the arms — keeping the rotation tight and explosive
👉 They plant feet shoulder-width apart at depth — not too close, not too deep
👉 They fire into a tight streamline before breaking the surface — dolphin kicks start underwater, not after

The scary part? Most of these fixes take one session to drill. 😳

Here's what this actually means for your times:
✔️ A cleaner approach preserves momentum going into the wall
✔️ **A properly executed turn can save 0.5–1 second per wall** — across 8 turns in a 400m, that's up to 8 seconds back
✔️ Jaked's compression fabric holds your streamline longer off the wall — so every push-off carries further

With NZ Age Group Champs racing TODAY, are you leaving time on the wall? 👇
facebook.engagement_bait: Drop a 💧 if you've been working on your turns — or tag the teammate whose flip turn needs a glow-up! 👇
